metadata.description: requests 库: `requests.get/post/put/delete`. Session for reusing connections. One of the most popular HTTP libraries. Python code examples: ```python import requests # Using GET request response = requests.get('https://api.example.com/data') print(response.status_code) # Using POST request with data data = {'key': 'value'} response = requests.post('https://api.example.com/submit', data=data) print(response.status_code) # Using PUT request headers = {'Content-Type': 'application/json'} json_data = {'id': 1, 'name': 'example'} response = requests.put('https://api.example.com/update', json=json_data, headers=headers) print(response.status_code) # Using DELETE request response = requests.delete('https://api.example.com/delete/1') print(response.status_code) # Session for reusing connections s = requests.Session() response = s.get('https://api.example.com/data') print(response.status_code) ``` This code demonstrates the use of various HTTP methods (`get`, `post`, `put`, and `delete`) with the `requests` library, as well as how to reuse a session for multiple requests.

data_pointers.content_preview: requests 库: `requests.get/post/put/delete`. Session for reusing connections. One of the most popular HTTP libraries. Python code examples: ```python import requests # Using GET request response = requests.get('https://api.example.com/data') print(response.status_code) # Using POST request with data data = {'key': 'value'} response = requests.post('https://api.example.com/submit', data=data) print(response.status_code) # Using PUT request headers = {'Content-Type': 'application/json'} json_data = {'id': 1, 'name': 'example'} response = requests.put('https://api.example.com/update', json=json_data, headers=headers) print(response.status_code) # Using DELETE request response = requests.delete('https://api.example.com/delete/1') print(response.status_code) # Session for reusing connections s = requests.Session() response = s.get('https://api.example.com/data') print(response.status_code) ``` This code demonstrates the use of various HTTP methods (`get`, `post`, `put`, and `delete`) with the `requests` library, as well as how to reuse a session for multiple requests.
